title: ⚔️ Chapter boss ⭐⭐⭐: permission, desire and place

options.text: I want visit the science lab.
I want to visit the science lab.
I want visiting the science lab.
I want to visiting the science lab.

questions.explanation: The pattern is "want + to + base verb": I want to visit the lab ✓. The common trap is "I want visit", which drops the "to". "Want visiting" and "want to visiting" wrongly use the -ing form after "want to".

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ water do you want in your bottle?"
questions.explanation: "Water" is uncountable (we cannot say "two waters"), so we ask with "How much": How much water do you want? "How many" is only for countable nouns like bottles or cups.

questions.prompt: Karim is strong, but the suitcase is enormous. Complete: "He ___ lift it alone."
questions.explanation: The suitcase is too big to lift alone, so Karim is NOT able to: he cannot lift it alone. "Can" says the opposite (he is able to), "must" is about obligation, and "wants" is about desire, not ability.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The eraser is ___ the ruler ___ the pencil-case."
questions.explanation: "Between" always goes with "and": between the ruler and the pencil-case ✓. The common trap is "between … or" — "between" is only followed by "and". "Under" and "behind" would change the meaning completely.

questions.prompt: Emma speaks English very well and without stopping. Complete: "She speaks English ___."
questions.explanation: To say HOW she speaks, we need an adverb of manner: adjective "fluent" + -ly = "fluently". She speaks fluently ✓. "Fluent" alone is the adjective, and "fluent way" / "more fluent" are not adverb forms.

questions.prompt: Read: "(1) Can I see your classroom? (2) Of course you can. (3) I want to visit the lab. (4) The headmaster can to help you." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: Sentence 4 has the mistake: this question asks you to find the faulty sentence. "The headmaster can to help you" is wrong: after "can" the verb never takes "to" — it must be "can help you" ✓. Sentences 1, 2 and 3 correctly use "Can I…?", "Of course you can" and "want to visit".
